message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracking state

Update beads issues tracking:
- bd-2cjt: demo_showcase interactive wizard now in_progress
- bd-3vf2: advanced content scenes assigned to SwiftWolf, in_progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-26ey","title":"demo_showcase snapshots: dashboard snapshot","description":"Add insta snapshot coverage for the dashboard in non-live snapshot mode (deterministic).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltCanyon","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:23.731755617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:21:14.995711860Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26ey","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:02.651296509Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26ey","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:10.039283884Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26ey","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:07.514833139Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26ey","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:18:05.071578049Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26ey","depends_on_id":"bd-yzr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:23.747481478Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":85,"issue_id":"bd-26ey","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot contract

Snapshot a *static* dashboard rendering (no live animation) so output is deterministic.

Implementation note: we may need a `--no-live` or explicit “snapshot mode” path that renders once.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-26gp","title":"demo_showcase: feature strategy (Cargo flags)","description":"Decide how demo_showcase relates to crate features (recommend `--features full,tracing` vs add a `showcase` alias vs `[[bin]].required-features`).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealCave","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:23.968961955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:46:28.082021959Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26gp","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:46.057307163Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-26gp","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:23.984489073Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":64,"issue_id":"bd-26gp","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Decision points

We need a clear story for “how to run the full demo”:

Option A (simplest):
- Document `--features full,tracing` and keep demo code feature-gated.

Option B (nice UX):
- Add a new feature alias, e.g. `showcase = ["full", "tracing"]`.
- Document `--features showcase`.

Option C (strict):
- Declare `[[bin]]` in Cargo.toml and set `required-features = ["full", "tracing"]` so the binary only builds when fully enabled.
- Risk: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` fails without extra flags (less friendly).

## Acceptance criteria

- New users can run the “best” demo with one obvious command.
- Default `cargo run --bin demo_showcase` should still do something reasonable (unless we explicitly choose strict required-features).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:33Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2b0s","title":"demo_showcase: narrative + scene storyboard","description":"Choose the demo storyline, name, copy, and the ordered scene list (full run + single-scene names).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.806761210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217861682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:10.217788947Z","close_reason":"Completed (spec in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:12.821701513Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-2b0s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Output deliverable

Produce a concrete storyboard so implementation can follow without ambiguity.

### Recommended scene list (example)

1. `hero` – branding + capabilities + palette preview
2. `dashboard` – live layout with services/pipeline/logs
3. `deep_dive_markdown` – release notes / runbook
4. `deep_dive_syntax` – config/code snippet
5. `deep_dive_json` – API payloads
6. `debug_tools` – pretty/inspect + traceback + logging/tracing
7. `export` – HTML/SVG bundle
8. `outro` – summary table/tree + next steps

### Naming

Pick a fictional product name and keep it consistent in copy, e.g. “Nebula Deploy”.

## Acceptance criteria

- Each scene has a 1–2 sentence purpose description.
- Each scene lists which rich_rust features it must exercise.
- Single-scene mode (`--scene <name>`) uses these names verbatim.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:29Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2cjt","title":"demo_showcase: interactive start wizard","description":"Add an optional interactive start wizard using Prompt (scenario/speed/export choices).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.004600090Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118079718Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","prompt"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:46.637019230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:49.118050083Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:44.192163526Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:20.017001830Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-2cjt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Wizard questions (suggested)

- Run mode: full demo vs specific scene
- Speed: normal vs quick
- Export: yes/no

Keep it short and skippable (defaults).

## Acceptance criteria

- Only runs when interactive and not disabled.
- Falls back to defaults otherwise.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:31Z"}]}

Keep it short and skippable (defaults).

## Acceptance criteria

- Falls back to defaults otherwise.

 {"id":"bd-2cyz","title":"demo_showcase snapshots: hero scene","description":"Add insta snapshot coverage for the hero/capabilities scene (deterministic output).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:21.282174155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:07:10.397781871Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:07:10.397705970Z","close_reason":"Hero scene snapshot already created in bd-cisk (demo_showcase_snapshots__hero_scene.snap)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","snapshots","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-11zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:55.072244250Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-3s5n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:57.482699738Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-5bjf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:59.965042092Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","depends_on_id":"bd-yzr8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:21.298095219Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-2cyz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Snapshot contract

Run `--scene hero` with deterministic flags (seed + fixed dimensions) and snapshot ANSI-stripped output.

Assertions:
- scene header present
- capability table/panel content present
- palette preview present
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:17:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2fl5","title":"demo_showcase: visual design system (theme/palette)","description":"Define a cohesive theme/palette and named styles that make the demo look like a real product.","notes":"Completed via child beads: bd-1by8 (theme/palette definitions in theme.rs) and bd-313x (typography patterns in typography.rs). Named styles include brand.*, status.*, section.*, panel.*, table.*, log.* prefixes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:17.976813041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629814050Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:43:16.629672817Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:24.788358650Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:18.006687094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"bd-2fl5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the demo visually consistent and “brandable” using the Theme system and rich_rust style primitives. This is where we turn the output from “works” into “wow”.

## Design principles

- Use a small, consistent palette: primary/accent/success/warn/error + neutrals.
- Prefer named styles via `Theme` so users can copy patterns (`status.ok`, `status.warn`, `panel.title`, etc.).
- Keep readability on low-color terminals: ensure acceptable downgrades to 16/256 colors.
- Use whitespace, rules, and alignment to create hierarchy (like a UI, not a log dump).

## Deliverables

- A `Theme::from_style_definitions(...)` set used by the demo’s Console.
- Named styles for:
  - headers / hero
  - section rules
  - table header + key columns
  - status badges (OK/WARN/ERR)
  - log levels
  - “call to action” links
- Optional: a small helper to render “badges” like `[bold white on green] OK [/ ]`.

## Acceptance criteria

- The demo uses named styles in *most* scenes (not just ad-hoc `Style::parse`).
- The demo still looks coherent when forced to `ColorSystem::Standard`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2hba","title":"demo_showcase: UI helper primitives","description":"Implement small UI helpers (badges, section headers, key/value rows) using Text/Style.","notes":"Added kv_row(), kv_row_styled(), badge() helpers to typography.rs. Combined with existing status_badge(), status_text(), section_header() from bd-313x, all requested UI primitives are now available.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.342641761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:16.161120916Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:16.160994450Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:23.099136270Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:01.357952838Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2hba","depends_on_id":"bd-313x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:51:25.537534583Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2hba","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Helpers to build

Small, reusable building blocks for the demo binary:
- `badge(text, style_name)` -> `Text` (or markup snippet)
- `kv_row(key, value)` -> renderable suitable for panels
- `section_title("...")` -> rule + aligned title

These keep the demo consistent and prevent copy/paste styling drift.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:19Z"}]}
@@ -164,7 +164,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-3rhg","title":"demo_showcase: interactive helpers integration","description":"Use Status/Prompt/Pager to make the demo feel like a real CLI wizard when running in a TTY.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.668932689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917382989Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","interactive"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-12nv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:56.917352933Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:54.500826925Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:55.684260804Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":15,"issue_id":"bd-3rhg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

Make the showcase feel like a real operator tool, not just output. Use the new interactive helpers *only when safe* (TTY):

- **Prompt**: ask for scenario / speed / whether to export
- **Status**: spinner while “preparing deployment plan” / “running checks”
- **Pager**: show long Markdown (runbook/release notes) without dumping everything into scrollback

## Safety

- If `Console::is_terminal()` is false, Prompt/Pager must not block.
- Provide `--no-interactive` to disable even in a TTY (useful for scripts).

## Acceptance criteria

- Interactive mode improves UX but is never required to run the demo.
- `--no-interactive` yields deterministic non-blocking output.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3s5n","title":"demo_showcase: CLI completeness pass (seed + links)","description":"Complete the CLI contract: add missing flags (--seed, --links/--no-links, etc.) and align --help output with the finalized spec.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:46.788764478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158199409Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T23:33:19.158139518Z","close_reason":"Completed: add --seed + --links flags (help/tests/README)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:46.801784165Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-3s5n","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

Early scaffolding landed a basic parser, but the *final* demo experience needs a few additional flags to support determinism + UX parity with the spec.

## Required additions

- `--seed <u64>`
  - used to seed any pseudo-random demo data (service IDs, jitter, stage durations)
  - default should be stable (e.g. 0 or fixed constant) so `cargo test` snapshots don't drift

- `--links` / `--no-links`
  - control OSC8 hyperlink emission independent of other styling

- Ensure `--help` and `--list-scenes` reflect the actual supported flags and scenes.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--seed` parses and rejects invalid values (non-integer, negative, etc.).
- Deterministic outputs are stable across runs when the same seed/width/height are used.
- Hyperlink control is explicit and defaults to a sensible value (likely auto-enabled when interactive).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3svf","title":"Full rendering pipeline integration tests","description":"## Problem
We need integration tests that verify the ENTIRE rendering pipeline from user API call to final output string. This would have caught the Cell markup bug.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test Console.print() with various renderables containing markup
- [ ] Capture actual output string and verify markup is processed
- [ ] Test Table -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test Panel -> Console -> StringBuffer -> verify styles
- [ ] Test nested renderables (Table in Panel, etc.) with markup

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Check that no raw markup tags like [bold] remain in output
- Verify ANSI escape codes are present for styled content
- These are TRUE integration tests - testing the full stack

## Files to modify
- Create new tests/integration/*.rs if needed
- Or add to tests/conformance/ with clear naming","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:07.703397468Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166416332Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T06:21:54.166109694Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added 5 full-pipeline integration tests to e2e_rendering.rs. Tests cover Table/Panel with markup, Cell::new vs from_markup differences, Console.print with markup, and nested markup styles. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
-{"id":"bd-3vf2","title":"demo_showcase: advanced content scenes (syntax/markdown/json)","description":"Showcase feature-gated renderables (Syntax/Markdown/Json) with a cohesive narrative (release notes, configs, payloads).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.544872637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:39.937293371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:39.937265379Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:37.537077494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.557411012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Demonstrate the optional, “power-user” renderables in a way that feels like a real CLI tool:

- **Markdown** (`--features markdown` / `full`): show release notes / runbook snippet with headings, lists, code fences.
- **Syntax** (`--features syntax` / `full`): show a config file or code snippet with line numbers and a theme selection.
- **JSON** (`--features json` / `full`): show API request/response payloads with pretty formatting and theming.

## Feature gating behavior

When a feature isn’t enabled, the scene must still run and print a friendly message that includes the exact command to enable it, e.g.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full`

## Acceptance criteria

- With `--features full`, the content scenes look excellent and integrate into the overall narrative (not disconnected examples).
- Without features, output is still polished: a small panel explaining what’s disabled and how to enable it (no `cfg`-related compile failures).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:19Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-3vf2","title":"demo_showcase: advanced content scenes (syntax/markdown/json)","description":"Showcase feature-gated renderables (Syntax/Markdown/Json) with a cohesive narrative (release notes, configs, payloads).","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SwiftWolf","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.544872637Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T04:36:52.043627371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["content","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:39.937265379Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:37.537077494Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:10.557411012Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-3vf2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Demonstrate the optional, “power-user” renderables in a way that feels like a real CLI tool:

- **Markdown** (`--features markdown` / `full`): show release notes / runbook snippet with headings, lists, code fences.
- **Syntax** (`--features syntax` / `full`): show a config file or code snippet with line numbers and a theme selection.
- **JSON** (`--features json` / `full`): show API request/response payloads with pretty formatting and theming.

## Feature gating behavior

When a feature isn’t enabled, the scene must still run and print a friendly message that includes the exact command to enable it, e.g.
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full`

## Acceptance criteria

- With `--features full`, the content scenes look excellent and integrate into the overall narrative (not disconnected examples).
- Without features, output is still polished: a small panel explaining what’s disabled and how to enable it (no `cfg`-related compile failures).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3w3u","title":"demo_showcase: feature-gate messaging","description":"Define exact user-facing messaging for missing features (syntax/markdown/json/tracing) and the commands to enable them.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:20.359674542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:48.512871956Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:38.562399412Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","depends_on_id":"bd-26gp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:56:48.512845356Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:20.371957033Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-3w3u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## User-facing messaging

When a feature is disabled at compile time, the demo should say so clearly and provide an exact command. Example pattern:

> This scene requires the `syntax` feature.
> Run: `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full`

Also include `tracing` guidance separately:
- `cargo run --bin demo_showcase --features full,tracing`

## Acceptance criteria

- Messaging is consistent across Markdown/Syntax/JSON/Tracing scenes.
- Output is still “pretty”: use a panel + named styles rather than plain `println!`.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4jzg","title":"demo_showcase: Status spinner moments","description":"Use Status spinner for a few short ‘working…’ moments to make the demo feel alive.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:22.516615870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:54.103634833Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","status"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:54.103605008Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","depends_on_id":"bd-1y6r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:53:51.512644921Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","depends_on_id":"bd-3rhg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:22.528925448Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-4jzg","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Where to use Status

Use sparingly for polish:
- “Validating environment…”
- “Building deployment plan…”
- “Generating export bundle…”

## Acceptance criteria

- In non-interactive mode, prints the message once (no animation).
- In interactive mode, animates briefly and stops cleanly.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4zjp","title":"Audit existing tests for trivial assertions","description":"## Problem
We found trivial assertions like assert!(x.is_empty() || true) and assert!(!output.is_empty() || output.is_empty()) that were always true and provided no value. There may be more lurking in the codebase.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Audit all test files for trivial assertions that are always true/false
- [ ] Audit for assertions that don't actually verify behavior (just check non-empty)
- [ ] Replace trivial assertions with meaningful ones
- [ ] Add proper style/content verification where output is captured

## Patterns to find
- assert!(x || true)
- assert!(x || !x) 
- assert!(!x.is_empty()) without checking content
- let _ = something; // Just checking it doesn't crash

## Files to audit
- tests/conformance/*.rs
- src/**/*.rs (inline tests)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:49.161430662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867626646Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:11:36.867605776Z","close_reason":"Replaced trivial assertions with meaningful content/width checks","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
